rationale: The citing paper explicitly states it "extends prior slow-motion research by shifting the focus from cognitive inferences (e.g., intentionality) to aesthetic and hedonic outcomes," which directly aligns with the cited paper’s core intentionality-inference mechanism in slow-motion advertising. Given this clear conceptual contrast/extension linkage (not a generic background mention) and a direct DOI match (10.1177/00222437211025054), this pair is likely substantively related.
